truth.papers.A41.title: Low-density nano-bainitic Al–C steels
truth.papers.A41.paper_scope: Investigates the bainitic transformation and mechanical performance of five medium-to high-Al steels with varying C contents, alloying elements (Ti, Ni), and austenitizing/austempering parameters.

prediction.papers.A41.title: Low-density nano-bainitic Al–C steels
prediction.papers.A41.paper_scope: Systematic investigation of bainitic transformation kinetics and mechanical performance of medium-to-high-Al steels with varying C contents, alloying elements, and heat treatment parameters, supported by MatCalc precipitation simulations.

truth.alloys.alloy_s1.alloys_notes: Designed with 0.98 wt% C and 6.00 wt% Al to minimize density and bainitic transformation time. Mo added for hardenability, B for further hardenability improvement.
truth.alloys.alloy_s2.alloys_notes: Slightly higher C content (1.10 wt%) than S1, otherwise similar design. Mo and B also added.
truth.alloys.alloy_s3.alloys_notes: Highest Al content (8.08 wt%), designed to explore very high Al effects on bainitic transformation and mechanical properties. Contains Mo and B.
truth.alloys.alloy_s4.alloys_notes: Ti-micro-alloyed steel with reduced C (0.87 wt%) and Al (5.76 wt%) to accelerate bainitic transformation and improve ductility. Ni (0.78 wt%) added to suppress polygonal ferrite and maintain an αp-free microstructure.
truth.alloys.alloy_s5.alloys_notes: Lower alloying concept with 0.60 wt% C and 3.92 wt% Al, Ni addition (0.78 wt%) to ensure αp-free microstructure without compromising bainitic transformation kinetics.

prediction.alloys.alloy_s1.alloys_notes: Designed with high C and Al to stabilize ferrite and accelerate bainitic transformation while maintaining Ms around 300 °C.
prediction.alloys.alloy_s2.alloys_notes: Higher C content than S1; otherwise similar high-Al concept.
prediction.alloys.alloy_s3.alloys_notes: Highest Al content (8.08 wt.%) in the series; exhibits strongly stabilized retained austenite and sluggish transformation kinetics.
prediction.alloys.alloy_s4.alloys_notes: Ti-microalloyed grade with slightly reduced C and Al; Ni added to prevent polygonal ferrite formation.
prediction.alloys.alloy_s5.alloys_notes: Lower C–Al concept with Ni addition; fastest bainitic transformation kinetics and near-stoichiometric composition for optimal transformation.

truth.processes.proc_1150_350.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C for 15 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 350 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1150_350.processes_notes: Heating above 1100 °C at 1 K/s; rapid cooling to suppress intermediate transformations; applied to S1, S2, S4, and S5 (exact duration for S5 austenitizing not specified, assumed similar).
truth.processes.proc_1150_300.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C for 15 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 300 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1150_300.processes_notes: Lower austempering temperature than 350 °C; used for S1 and S2.
truth.processes.proc_1220_350.description: Austenitizing at 1220 °C for 20 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 350 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1220_350.processes_notes: Higher austenitizing temperature required for S3 to minimize polygonal ferrite.
truth.processes.proc_1220_270.description: Austenitizing at 1220 °C for 20 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 270 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1220_270.processes_notes: Austempering just above Ms (258 °C) for S3; leads to very long transformation time.
truth.processes.proc_1150_320.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C (duration not explicitly specified, presumably 15 min), cooling at 100 K/s to 320 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1150_320.processes_notes: Used for S4 to explore an intermediate austempering temperature.
truth.processes.proc_1250_350.description: Austenitizing at 1250 °C for 15 min, cooling at 100 K/s to 350 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1250_350.processes_notes: Higher austenitizing temperature for S4 to dissolve TiC/MC precipitates and reduce impingement.
truth.processes.proc_1150_365.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C (duration not explicitly specified, presumed 15 min), cooling at 100 K/s to 365 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1150_365.processes_notes: High austempering temperature for S5, above Ms (331 °C).
truth.processes.proc_1150_335.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C (duration not explicitly specified, presumed 15 min), cooling at 100 K/s to 335 °C, isothermal holding until bainitic transformation finish.
truth.processes.proc_1150_335.processes_notes: Austempering just above Ms (331 °C) for S5; promotes fine bainitic structure.

prediction.processes.proc_1150_300.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 300 °C to produce nano-bainitic microstructure in S1 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_1150_350.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 350 °C to produce nano-bainitic microstructure in S1 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_s2_300.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 300 °C to produce nano-bainitic microstructure in S2 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_s2_350.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 350 °C to produce nano-bainitic microstructure in S2 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_1220_270.description: Austenitizing at 1220 °C followed by austempering at 270 °C to produce nano-bainitic microstructure in S3 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_1220_270.processes_notes: Required elevated austenitizing temperature due to high Al; polygonal ferrite persisted even at 1220 °C.
prediction.processes.proc_1220_350.description: Austenitizing at 1220 °C followed by austempering at 350 °C to produce nano-bainitic microstructure in S3 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_1220_350.processes_notes: Required elevated austenitizing temperature due to high Al; polygonal ferrite persisted even at 1220 °C.
prediction.processes.proc_1150_320.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 320 °C for Ti-microalloyed S4 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_s4_1150_350.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 350 °C for Ti-microalloyed S4 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_1250_350.description: Austenitizing at 1250 °C followed by austempering at 350 °C for Ti-microalloyed S4 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_1250_350.processes_notes: High austenitizing temperature chosen to dissolve nanoscale MC/TiC precipitates and reduce impingement effects.
prediction.processes.proc_1150_335.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 335 °C for lower-alloy S5 steel.
prediction.processes.proc_1150_365.description: Austenitizing at 1150 °C followed by austempering at 365 °C for lower-alloy S5 steel.

truth.processing_steps.sample_s1_1150_350.proc: Transformation finish time determined from dilatometric plateau.
truth.processing_steps.sample_s3_1220_350.proc: Selected to minimize polygonal ferrite, though some αp remained (~8.2%).
truth.processing_steps.sample_s3_1220_270.proc: Near-complete transformation required 43200 s; at 25200 s transformation was still incomplete.
truth.processing_steps.sample_s4_1150_350.meth: isothermal holding at 350 °C for 3000 s (Bt) after 1150 °C austenitization
truth.processing_steps.sample_s4_1150_350.proc: Duration not explicitly given for S4; assumed 15 min based on S1/S2.
truth.processing_steps.sample_s4_1150_320.meth: isothermal holding at 320 °C for 3500 s (Bt) after 1150 °C austenitization
truth.processing_steps.sample_s4_1250_350.meth: isothermal holding at 350 °C for 2600 s (Bt) after 1250 °C austenitization
truth.processing_steps.sample_s4_1250_350.proc: Selected to dissolve TiC/MC precipitates and reduce impingement from fine prior austenite grains.
truth.processing_steps.sample_s5_1150_365.proc: Duration not explicitly stated; assumed 15 min.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_s1_1150_300.proc: Incubation time: 80 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 4000 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s1_1150_350.proc: Incubation time: 100 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 1600 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s2_1150_300.proc: Incubation time: 130 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 3600 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s2_1150_350.proc: Incubation time: 210 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 2300 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s3_1220_270.proc: Incubation time: 120 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 43,000 s; slow bainite growth period lasted nearly 3000 s after incubation.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s3_1220_350.proc: Incubation time: 140 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 14,000 s; slow bainite growth period lasted nearly 1500 s after incubation.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s4_1150_320.proc: Incubation time: 50–60 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 3500 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s4_1150_350.proc: Incubation time: 50–60 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 3000 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s4_1250_350.proc: Incubation time: 50–60 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 2600 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s5_1150_335.proc: Incubation time: 10 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 700 s.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_s5_1150_365.proc: Incubation time: 10 s; bainitic transformation finish time (Bt): 1000 s.

truth.structures.sample_s1_1150_350.overall_st: Nano-bainitic structure with bainitic ferrite laths, retained austenite (γr) as films and blocks, and (Fe,M)xCy precipitates near phase boundaries.
truth.structures.sample_s1_1150_350.microstruc: lath-like, nano-bainitic, interlocked Type 1 and Type 2 lamellae
film-like at lath boundaries and blocky regions between bainite packets
rounded, located near γr/αb boundaries; no micro-void formation observed
truth.structures.sample_s1_1150_300.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with finer laths, retained austenite, and similar precipitates.
truth.structures.sample_s1_1150_300.microstruc: lath-like, average true thickness Lt(t) 35 nm
film-like, lower volume fraction compared to 350 °C treatment
truth.structures.sample_s2_1150_350.overall_st: Nano-bainitic structure with slightly coarser laths (Lt(t) 68 nm), high retained austenite (34%), and (Fe,M)xCy precipitates.
truth.structures.sample_s2_1150_350.microstruc: lath-like, Type 1 and Type 2, average Lt(t) 68 nm
truth.structures.sample_s2_1150_300.overall_st: Fine nano-bainite with Lt(t) 34 nm, Vγ 29%, microstrain εγ 0.47%, εα 0.44%.
truth.structures.sample_s3_1220_350.overall_st: Dual-phase structure with polygonal ferrite (αp ~8.2%), nano-bainite, and retained austenite. Sluggish γr observed.
truth.structures.sample_s3_1220_350.microstruc: allotriomorphic and occasional idiomorphic plates; α or δ nature unclear, referred to as αp
mixed film-like and blocky; sluggish transformation regions with C-enriched γr near αp
truth.structures.sample_s3_1220_270.overall_st: Fine bainitic ferrite (Lt(t) 40 nm) with retained austenite (29%) and pronounced strain partitioning (εγ 0.54%, εα 0.33%). αp present. Ductility severely reduced.
truth.structures.sample_s3_1220_270.microstruc: allotriomorphic, similar to 350 °C condition but volume fraction not explicitly given; αp persists
lath-like, Lt(t) 40 nm; higher micro-strain in γr (0.54%) than in αb (0.33%)
mixed, with partially decomposed sluggish γr near αp boundaries
dislocation accumulation in γr leading to strain partitioning
truth.structures.sample_s4_1150_350.overall_st: Nano-bainite with TiMoC precipitates aligned in rolling direction, minimal αp (<1%), and reduced (Fe,M)xCy.
truth.structures.sample_s4_1150_350.microstruc: lath-like, Lt(t) 56 nm; impingement of bainite sheaves observed
longitudinally aligned, indicating formation during cooling after casting and subsequent hot rolling deformation
elongated clusters along prior deformation direction; no micro-defects observed around them
truth.structures.sample_s4_1150_320.overall_st: Nano-bainite with TiMoC precipitates, Lt(t) 36 nm, Vγ 26%.
truth.structures.sample_s4_1250_350.overall_st: Nano-bainite with TiMoC and reduced impingement due to higher austenitizing temperature; Lt(t) 58 nm, Vγ 29%.
truth.structures.sample_s4_1250_350.microstruc: elongated, partly dissolved at 1250 °C but re-precipitated during cooling
truth.structures.sample_s5_1150_365.overall_st: Bainitic structure with slightly coarser laths (Lt(t) 55 nm), film-like γr, and trace allotriomorphic ferrite (F). No martensite. Rapid transformation, minimal impingement.
truth.structures.sample_s5_1150_365.microstruc: lath-like packets, slightly coarser than at 335 °C; KAM map shows marginally higher global KAM
predominantly film-like at lath boundaries; no blocky γr reported
allotriomorphic ferrite (labelled F in EBSD), very minor
truth.structures.sample_s5_1150_335.overall_st: Fine nano-bainite with Lt(t) 41 nm, lenticular martensite islands scattered, lower γr (19%), and higher microstrain in γr (0.51%). Local KAM spikes near M.
truth.structures.sample_s5_1150_335.microstruc: narrow lath packets; KAM hot-spots at boundaries and near martensite
film-like; volume fraction undercounted by EBSD, XRD value 19%
sporadic lenticular plates; form stress-assisted transformation slightly above Ms (331 °C)
geometrically necessary dislocations near M and lath boundaries

prediction.structures.sample_s1_1150_300.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite, produced by low-temperature austempering.
prediction.structures.sample_s1_1150_350.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite and broader lamellar distribution.
prediction.structures.sample_s2_1150_350.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite and heterogeneous lamellar sizes.
prediction.structures.sample_s3_1220_270.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite, polygonal ferrite, and pronounced strain partitioning.
prediction.structures.sample_s3_1220_270.microstruc: sluggish γr in partially decomposed regions
prediction.structures.sample_s3_1220_350.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite and persistent polygonal ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_s4_1150_320.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite and reduced impingement.
prediction.structures.sample_s4_1150_350.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite and TiMoC precipitates.
prediction.structures.sample_s4_1150_350.microstruc: along phase and grain boundaries; longitudinal alignment indicates formation during cooling after casting with subsequent hot deformation
prediction.structures.sample_s4_1250_350.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite and reduced precipitate impingement due to higher austenitizing temperature.
prediction.structures.sample_s4_1250_350.microstruc: along phase and grain boundaries; reduced impingement compared to 1150 °C condition
prediction.structures.sample_s5_1150_335.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite, fine bainitic packets, and sporadic lenticular martensite.
prediction.structures.sample_s5_1150_335.microstruc: nanoscale bainitic lamellae in narrow packets
εγ: 0.51 %, εα: 0.45 %; local KAM hotspots near martensite
prediction.structures.sample_s5_1150_365.overall_st: Nano-bainitic microstructure with retained austenite and trace allotriomorphic ferrite.
prediction.structures.sample_s5_1150_365.microstruc: nanoscale bainitic lamellae in slightly coarser packets

truth.interfaces.sample_s1_1150_350.phase_evol: γr films at bainitic lath boundaries provide TRIP effect; (Fe,M)xCy precipitates at interfaces do not induce micro-voids and have negligible negative effect.
truth.interfaces.sample_s3_1220_350.interface_: C-partitioning from αp into adjacent γr locally stabilizes austenite and inhibits bainite growth, leading to sluggish transformation and incomplete reaction in some regions.
truth.interfaces.sample_s4_1150_350.phases[tim: favorable wettability, no micro-defects at interface
truth.interfaces.sample_s4_1150_350.interface_: Impingement of bainite sheaves due to increased phase boundaries from TiMoC precipitates; this limits acceleration of transformation after nucleation.
truth.interfaces.sample_s5_1150_335.defect_int: high GND density at M interfaces
Local KAM hot-spots adjacent to martensite indicate stress concentration and geometrically necessary dislocations.

truth.properties.sample_s1_1150_350.mechanical: not explicitly given; generally correlated with UTS MPa

truth.characterization_methods.sample_s1_1150_: SEM, EDX, XRD, LOM, EBSD, dilatometry
Microstructure characterization, phase quantification, precipitate analysis, transformation kinetics
Nano-bainitic structure with Lt(t) 55 nm, Vγ 34%, εγ 0.35%, εα 0.45%. (Fe,M)xCy precipitates observed at γr/αb boundaries without micro-voids.
Finer bainite (35 nm), Vγ 27%, higher hardness.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_s2_1150_: Coarser laths (68 nm), Vγ 34%, similar precipitates.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_s3_1220_: SEM, EDX, XRD, LOM, EBSD, dilatometry
αp ~8.2%, bainite 58 nm, Vγ 34%, sluggish transformation regions.
Bainite 40 nm, Vγ 29%, strong strain partitioning (εγ 0.54%, εα 0.33%), ductility drop.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_s4_1150_: SEM, EDX, XRD, LOM, EBSD, dilatometry
TiMoC precipitates longitudinally aligned, bainite 56 nm, Vγ 25%, impingement observed.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_s5_1150_: Bainite 55 nm, Vγ 26%, film-like γr, trace allotriomorphic ferrite, no martensite.
Bainite 41 nm, Vγ 19%, lenticular martensite locally, γr microstrain 0.51%, KAM hot-spots near M.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s1_1150_: Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
SEM: bainitic lamellar thickness Lt(t) = 35 ± 11 nm. XRD: retained austenite Vγ = 27 %, εγ = 0.50 %, εα = 0.45 %. SEM/EDX: (Fe,M)xCy precipitates observed near phase boundaries.
Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
LOM: αp-free. SEM: Lt(t) = 55 ± 23 nm. XRD: Vγ = 34 %, εγ = 0.35 %, εα = 0.45 %. SEM/EDX: (Fe,M)xCy precipitates with Si and Mo entrapment.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s2_1150_: Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
SEM: Lt(t) = 34 ± 11 nm. XRD: Vγ = 29 %, εγ = 0.47 %, εα = 0.44 %. SEM/EDX: (Fe,M)xCy precipitates observed.
Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
LOM: αp-free. SEM: Lt(t) = 68 ± 28 nm. XRD: Vγ = 34 %, εγ = 0.45 %, εα = 0.44 %. SEM/EDX: (Fe,M)xCy precipitates observed.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s3_1220_: Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
LOM: αp = 8.2 % present. SEM: sluggish γr and incomplete transformation regions. Lt(t) = 40 ± 10 nm. XRD: Vγ = 29 %, εγ = 0.54 %, εα = 0.33 %. SEM/EDX: (Fe,M)xCy precipitates and AlN detected.
Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
LOM: αp = 8.2 %. SEM: Lt(t) = 58 ± 23 nm. XRD: Vγ = 34 %, εγ = 0.55 %, εα = 0.50 %. SEM/EDX: (Fe,M)xCy precipitates confirmed.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s4_1150_: Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
SEM: Lt(t) = 36 ± 12 nm. XRD: Vγ = 26 %, εγ = 0.58 %, εα = 0.60 %. SEM/EDX: TiMoC complex carbides and AlN present; TiMoC shows Fe depletion.
Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
LOM: αp < 1.0 %. SEM: Lt(t) = 56 ± 18 nm. XRD: Vγ = 25 %, εγ = 0.31 %, εα = 0.44 %. SEM/EDX: TiMoC and AlN detected; longitudinal TiMoC alignment indicates primary precipitation during casting.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s4_1250_: Microstructural characterization, phase fraction and micro-strain determination
SEM: Lt(t) = 58 ± 18 nm. XRD: Vγ = 29 %, εγ = 0.35 %, εα = 0.44 %. SEM/EDX: TiMoC and AlN present; reduced impingement compared to 1150 °C austenitizing.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_s5_1150_: Microstructural and crystallographic characterization including local misorientation mapping

SEM: Lt(t) = 41 ± 14 nm, sporadic lenticular martensite (M). XRD: Vγ = 19 %, εγ = 0.51 %, εα = 0.45 %, no resolvable martensite peaks. EBSD IPF: narrow bainitic packets. EBSD KAM: local hotspots adjacent to M, average KAM ~0.825°.
Microstructural and crystallographic characterization including local misorientation mapping

LOM: αp < 1.0 %, trace allotriomorphic ferrite (F). SEM: Lt(t) = 55 ± 15 nm. XRD: Vγ = 26 %, εγ = 0.42 %, εα = 0.43 %. EBSD IPF: slightly coarser bainitic packets. EBSD KAM: marginally higher global average KAM ~0.905°.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Precipitation kinetics simulation (TTP diagrams) and equilibrium phase calculations using MatCalc software (v6.01). Based on heterogeneous nucleation theory, ortho-equilibrium transformation, considering dislocation and grain boundary sites. Volumetric misfit considered. Phases: fcc, bcc, liquid, κ-carbide, hcp carbide, AlN, TiCN–TiMoC system.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Microstructural precipitate analysis confirmed primary formation of (Fe,M)xCy and TiMoC; simulations supported findings, indicating primary precipitation above austempering temperatures at austenitizing and cooling stages.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: MatCalc equilibrium phase calculations and precipitation kinetics (TTP) simulations using step calculation method
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.software_use: MatCalc (Version: 6.01 (rel 1.003), Institute of Materials Science and Technology, TU Wien, Wien, Austria)
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Equilibrium phase fractions and TTP curves validated against experimental dilatometry, LOM, and XRD measurements
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Volumetric misfit considered; ortho-equilibrium transformation; nucleation sites limited to dislocations and grain boundaries; κ-carbides treated as fixed phase fractions due to spinodal decomposition
Synergy of fcc, bcc, liquid, κ-carbide, hcp carbide, AlN, and TiCN–TiMoC system considered for equilibrium and kinetic calculations

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Density reduction by Al: approximately 1.3% per 1 wt.% Al addition (from literature); not directly measured for these alloys.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Table 2 compiles MatCalc-predicted, measured, and applied TA temperatures and Ms values for each steel.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: For S3 at TB 270 °C, bainitic transformation required 43,200 s; an intermediate microstructure at 25,200 s still showed incomplete transformation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: LOM images at 1050 °C benchmark show αp retention in S1, S3, S4, S5; S2 is αp-free at this temperature.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Figure captions indicate extensive microscopy (Fig. 9,10,11) and simulation results (Fig. 4,8) that could not be fully captured due to lack of image pixel data; trends and labeled features are included where explicitly stated in captions or text.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Martensite start temperatures (Ms): S1 277 °C, S2 274 °C, S3 258 °C, S4 305 °C (1150 °C) / 310 °C (1250 °C), S5 331 °C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Product of strength and elongation (PSE) values: S1 at 300 °C 10,880 MPa·% and at 350 °C ~12,000 MPa·%; S2 at 300 °C 11,130 MPa·% and at 350 °C 12,970 MPa·%; S3 at 350 °C 11,150 MPa·% and at 270 °C ~3,480 MPa·%; S4 at 1250/350 °C 11,700 MPa·%, at 1150/320 °C 13,380 MPa·%, and at 1150/350 °C 13,600 MPa·%; S5 at 335 °C 10,710 MPa·% and at 365 °C 11,450 MPa·%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: HV30 hardness values across all studied alloys ranged between 450 and 500 HV30, generally higher at lower austempering temperatures except for S4.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: MatCalc-predicted ferrite-free austenitizing temperatures: S1 991 °C, S2 929 °C, S3 1177 °C, S4 1016 °C, S5 986 °C; measured operational TA: S1 1062 °C, S2 1031 °C, S3 broadened transition, S4 1067 °C, S5 ~900 °C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: TTP simulations indicated that (Fe,M)xCy precipitation during cooling is unavoidable in S1 and S3 between approximately 700 °C and 1150 °C, and in S5 between 700 °C and 1000 °C; in S4, initial (Fe,M)xCy nucleation is suppressed by the counter-coarsening effect of the Ti–Mo–C precipitate system.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: For S3, a slow bainite growth period following incubation lasted nearly 3000 s at 270 °C and 1500 s at 350 °C, attributed to carbon enrichment around pre-existing polygonal ferrite stabilizing adjacent austenite and increasing the energy barrier for transformation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: EDX analysis confirmed (Fe,M)xCy precipitates in S1–S3 contain Si and Mo due to entrapment during rapid cooling; S4 exhibits TiMoC complex carbides with Fe depletion and AlN particles.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: EBSD KAM maps for S5 show average kernel average misorientation of ~0.825° at 335 °C and ~0.905° at 365 °C, with local hotspots adjacent to lenticular martensite at 335 °C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: S5 at 335 °C exhibited sporadic lenticular martensite plates (Ms + 4 K) compatible with stress-assisted transformation; XRD showed no resolvable martensite peaks, implying a volume fraction below the diffractometric detectability limit.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Bainitic transformation finish times (Bt) determined by dilatometry: S1 1600 s (350 °C) and 4000 s (300 °C); S2 2300 s (350 °C) and 3600 s (300 °C); S3 14,000 s (350 °C) and 43,000 s (270 °C); S4 3000 s (1150/350 °C), 3500 s (1150/320 °C), 2600 s (1250/350 °C); S5 700 s (335 °C) and 1000 s (365 °C).
